text: PM RAHAT (Road Accident Holistic Action for Treatment): Launched in Feb 2026, this scheme provides cashless treatment for road accident victims. Benefits: Cashless treatment of up to ₹1.5 lakh per victim. Eligibility: All road accident victims on national and state highways in India.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

text: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY Senior Citizen Expansion (2026): A major update to the world's largest health insurance scheme. Benefits: Health coverage of ₹5 lakh per family annually. Eligibility: All senior citizens in India aged 70 years and above, regardless of income or economic status. Requirement: Aadhaar-based registration.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

text: Lakhpati Didi Initiative (2026 Expansion): Empowerment program for rural women. Benefits: Financial literacy, skill training, and market access to help women earn at least ₹1 lakh per year. Target: 6 crore women beneficiaries by 2029. Eligibility: Rural women members of Self-Help Groups (SHGs).

Divyang Sahara Yojana (2026): Support for persons with disabilities. Benefits: Financial assistance for assistive devices (wheelchairs, hearing aids, smart canes) and a monthly disability pension. Eligibility: Persons with 40% or more disability belonging to low-income households.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment

text: Startup India Fund of Funds 2.0 (FFS 2.0): A ₹10,000 crore corpus to boost the startup ecosystem. Benefits: Capital support to SEBI-registered Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs) which in turn invest in startups. Focus: Manufacturing, deep-tech, and green energy sectors.

PM-KUSUM (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Urja Suraksha evam Utthaan Mahabhiyan): Solar power for farmers. Benefits: Subsidies for installing solar pumps and solarizing existing grid-connected pumps. Farmers can also sell surplus power to the grid for extra income.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy

text: PM SVANidhi (Pradhan Mantri Street Vendor’s AtmaNirbhar Nidhi): Micro-credit for street vendors. Benefits: Collateral-free working capital loans starting from ₹10,000, with a second loan up to ₹20,000 and third up to ₹50,000 on timely repayment. Interest subsidy of 7%.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs

text: Bharat-VISTAAR (2026): Multilingual AI agricultural platform. Benefits: Real-time, customized advisory for crops, weather, and market prices in 22 regional languages. Uses generative AI to answer farmer queries directly.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare

text: PM Vishwakarma Scheme: Support for traditional artisans. Benefits: Recognition through PM Vishwakarma Certificate, skill upgrading, toolkit incentive of ₹15,000, and credit support up to ₹3 lakh at 5% interest rate.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises

text: Janani Suraksha Yojana (JSY): Maternal health scheme. Benefits: Cash assistance for institutional delivery to reduce maternal and neonatal mortality. Focuses on poor pregnant women in low-performing states.

metadata.ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare
